summary: The reproduction confirms that the WebSocket server pattern used in cosmos/evm rpc/websockets.go:101-102 (bare http.ListenAndServe without timeouts) allows Slowloris connections to persist indefinitely, while the HTTP server pattern from server/json_rpc.go:86-93 (http.Server with ReadHeaderTimeout/ReadTimeout/WriteTimeout/IdleTimeout) properly closes slow connections after the configured timeout.
expected_security_impact: A server using http.ListenAndServe without timeouts should keep Slowloris connections alive indefinitely (no ReadHeaderTimeout). A server using http.Server with 3-second timeouts should close slow connections within 3 seconds. The actual cosmos/evm source at rpc/websockets.go:102 contains http.ListenAndServe(s.wsAddr, ws) with a #nosec G114 suppression, confirming the vulnerable pattern.
actual_observed_result: The vulnerable server kept the Slowloris connection alive for 5+ seconds (well beyond any reasonable timeout). The safe server with 3-second timeouts closed the connection after exactly 3.0 seconds. Source code analysis confirmed that rpc/websockets.go uses http.ListenAndServe (line 102) without any timeout configuration, while server/json_rpc.go properly configures all four HTTP timeouts (ReadHeaderTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout at lines 89-92). The #nosec G114 suppression at line 101 explicitly acknowledges the missing timeout.
verification_method: The exploit opens TCP connections to both a vulnerable server (http.ListenAndServe, no timeouts) and a safe server (http.Server with 3s timeouts), sends partial HTTP requests (missing final CRLF), and observes: (1) vulnerable server keeps the connection alive past the expected timeout period, (2) safe server closes the connection within the configured timeout. Additionally, the exploit reads the actual cosmos/evm source code from /target-repo to confirm the vulnerable pattern exists at rpc/websockets.go:102.
evidence_produced_by_run: Vulnerable server connection alive after 5 seconds - no timeout enforced (Slowloris successful)
Safe server closed connection after 3.0 seconds - timeout enforced correctly
Source code verification: rpc/websockets.go contains http.ListenAndServe at line 102
Source code verification: rpc/websockets.go has #nosec G114 suppression acknowledging missing timeout
Source code verification: rpc/websockets.go does NOT set ReadHeaderTimeout
Source code verification: server/json_rpc.go sets all four HTTP timeouts (ReadHeaderTimeout, ReadTimeout, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout)
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction uses a standalone Go server that replicates the exact http.ListenAndServe pattern from the vulnerable code, rather than building and running the full cosmos/evm node. Building the full node requires Go 1.23+, all cosmos-sdk dependencies, and a testnet setup which takes significant time and network bandwidth.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
